experiment_types: Affinity purification coupled with mass spectrometry proteomics

publications.ref: Hou Y, Qiu J, Tong X, Wei X, Nallamilli BR, Wu W, Huang S, Zhang J. A comprehensive quantitative phosphoproteome analysis of rice in response to bacterial blight. BMC Plant Biol. 2015 Jun 26;15:163

Characteristics[Organism].evidence: Rice is a major crop worldwide... quantitative phosphoproteome analysis of rice

Characteristics[MaterialType].evidence: From PRIDE database anchor data and rice phosphoproteome context

Characteristics[Disease].evidence: Bacterial blight (BB) caused by Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae (Xoo) has become one of the most devastating diseases for rice

Characteristics[Treatment].evidence: before and 24 h after Xoo infection... 0 h and 24 h after Xoo infection

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].value: Affinity purification coupled with mass spectrometry proteomics
Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: From PRIDE database anchor data - phosphopeptide enrichment study

Characteristics[Label].reasoning: PRIDE anchor shows TIC quantification method, commonly associated with label-free approaches in phosphoproteomics
Characteristics[Label].needs: Explicit mention of labeling strategy in methods section

Characteristics[Compound].reasoning: Phosphoproteome studies typically require phosphatase inhibitors and phosphopeptide enrichment reagents

STEP1_OVERVIEW.summary: This is a rice phosphoproteomics study examining bacterial blight disease response. Confirmed organism (Oryza sativa), disease (bacterial blight), treatment (Xoo infection at 0h and 24h timepoints), and leaf tissue from anchor data. The experiment uses affinity purification MS with TIC quantification. Key unknowns include specific labeling strategy, rice strain/variety, and detailed sample preparation compounds.

Characteristics[CleavageAgent].evidence: The reduced and alkylated proteins were digested on the 30 kDa filter unit (Millipore) over night with trypsin at pH8.0 (with an enzyme to protein ratio of 1:50)

Characteristics[AlkylationReagent].evidence: alkylated with 20 mM IAA in dark for 30 min

Characteristics[ReductionReagent].evidence: Protein were first reduced with 5 mM DTT in 56 °C for 30 min

Characteristics[Label].evidence: No labeling reagents mentioned, quantification based on intensity comparison: 'The differentially phosphorylated protein was defined to have over two fold changes in the normalized average intensity'

Characteristics[Modification].evidence: Oxidation (M), Acetyl (Protein-N term), and Phospho (S/T/Y) was chosen as variable modifications, Carbamidomethyl (C) as fixed modification

Characteristics[Modification].1.evidence: Oxidation (M), Acetyl (Protein-N term), and Phospho (S/T/Y) was chosen as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].2.evidence: Oxidation (M), Acetyl (Protein-N term), and Phospho (S/T/Y) was chosen as variable modifications

Comment[EnrichmentMethod].evidence: The digested peptides were resolved with binding buffer (80 % ACN, 5 % TFA, 1 M lac acid), then incubated with TiO2 beads (GL sciences, peptide to TiO2 ratio of 1:4) for three times

Characteristics[Compound].reasoning: Lysis buffer contained '1X phosphoprotein protease inhibitor complex, and 1 mM phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride' which are compounds used in sample preparation
Characteristics[Compound].needs: Clarification on whether these should be listed as compounds or are part of standard buffer composition

STEP2_SAMPLE_PREP.summary: Sample preparation details confirmed: trypsin digestion with DTT reduction and IAA alkylation using FASP protocol. Label-free quantification confirmed with four modification types (Oxidation, Acetyl, Phospho as variable; Carbamidomethyl as fixed). TiO2 enrichment method confirmed for phosphopeptide capture. No fractionation or additional separation methods beyond LC-MS/MS. Previous Label hypothesis resolved as label-free.

Comment[FragmentationMethod].evidence: Q Exactive Plus instrument uses HCD fragmentation

Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer].evidence: Q Exactive Plus uses orbitrap analyzer for MS2

Comment[IonizationType].evidence: Q Exactive Plus with Easy-nLC1000 system uses nanoESI

Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance].evidence: The mass tolerance was set to be 20 ppm for precursor

Comment[FragmentMassTolerance].evidence: it was set 50mmu for the tolerance of product ions

Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages].evidence: one missed cleavage on trypsin was allowed

Comment[GradientTime].evidence: eluted in a 1 h 5-30 % acetonitrile gradient

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].value: Affinity purification coupled with mass spectrometry proteomics
Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: TiO2 phosphopeptide enrichment followed by LC-MS/MS analysis

Comment[FlowRateChromatogram].reasoning: Easy-nLC1000 with 75μm ID column typically operates at nanoflow rates, but specific flow rate not mentioned in text
Comment[FlowRateChromatogram].needs: explicit flow rate specification in methods

Comment[CollisionEnergy].reasoning: Q Exactive Plus typically uses normalized collision energy, but specific values not provided in manuscript
Comment[CollisionEnergy].needs: instrument method parameters or acquisition settings details

STEP3_MS_SETTINGS.summary: Mass spectrometry instrumentation confirmed as Q Exactive Plus with HCD fragmentation, orbitrap analyzer, and nanoESI ionization. Key search parameters established: 20 ppm precursor tolerance, 50 mmu fragment tolerance, 1 missed cleavage allowed. LC gradient confirmed as 1 hour 5-30% acetonitrile. Flow rate and collision energy settings remain unspecified in the manuscript text.

Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].evidence: Three individual biological replicates were used for each time point. Filename tokens 'test1', 'test2', 'test3' correspond to biological replicates.

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic.evidence: Three individual biological replicates were used for each time point

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples].evidence: 2 time points (0h, 24h) × 3 biological replicates = 6 total samples, matching the 6 raw files provided

FactorValue[Treatment].evidence: around 5 cm long IRBB5 leaves close to the clip position were collected immediately after Xoo inoculation (0 h) and at 24 h after inoculation (24 h). Filename tokens '0h' and '24h' clearly indicate time points.

FactorValue[Disease].evidence: IRBB5 and IRBB13 plants were inoculated with the Chinese representative strain of Xoo (Zhe173) at the booting stage

FactorValue[Compound].evidence: IRBB5 and IRBB13 plants were inoculated with the Chinese representative strain of Xoo (Zhe173)

FactorValue[ConcentrationOfCompound].1.evidence: The concentrations of Xoo suspension is up to 3x108 cfu/mL

Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica.reasoning: Each biological replicate appears to have one corresponding raw file, suggesting no technical replicates, but this is not explicitly stated
Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica.needs: Explicit statement about technical replicate strategy

STEP4_RAW_FILES.summary: Raw file analysis successfully mapped experimental design: 6 files represent 3 biological replicates at 2 time points (0h, 24h post-Xoo inoculation). Clear filename tokens enabled confident assignment of biological replicates (test1-3) and treatment time points (0h/24h). Confirmed bacterial blight disease model with Xoo strain Zhe173 at 3×10^8 cfu/mL concentration. Technical replicate strategy remains unclear from available information.
